name: Meta Card Backpack Legend
developer: 肉鸽鸽独立游戏工作室
publisher: 肉鸽鸽独立游戏工作室
short_description: The third installment of the MetaCard series, MetaCard: Backpack Legend, is a backpack management Roguelike card game! Before Battle: Manage your artifact backpack to build and synergize your playstyle. During Battle: Drag and drop cards to unleash skills on key enemies at the right moment.
detailed_description:
World's First Card + Backpack System

Cards + Backpack Management, Pre-battle + Mid-battle = Dual Strategy

Micro-control + Auto Combat: Enjoy explosive battles without operational burden!

1. Strategic Real-time Combat

New Artifact Backpack & Bond System enhance pre-battle depth!

Diverse card mechanics + unique character energy systems secure mid-battle tactics!

Bullet Time ensures decision space – no frantic button-mashing.

2. 1+1>2 Dual-Character System

True dual characters: Independent hands, simultaneous dual-line operation.

Synergy-triggered Emergence enables 1+1>2 combos:

Gale Slash + Black Hole = Tornado

Ice Arrow + Tornado = Blizzard

Fireball + Ice Arrow = Magma

Tornado + Lightning = Thunder Twister

Thunder Twister + Lightning Orb = Violet Thunder Dragon

More combos await discovery!

3. Farmable Roguelike

Repeatable stages + Non-repeating Artifact drops = Target-farm your build!

Your mastery of Backpack Management & Artifact synergy defines the ceiling – not RNG.

discord：
supported_languages: English, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, Korean

reviews.review_text: Classic backpack management, classic card building, and fusion monsters are just fun. The game has many mechanisms that are very strategic. Sometimes, due to the abundance of mechanisms, different equipment and accessories can be overwhelming, and the game pace is a bit slow. However, for card game enthusiasts, none of these are issues.
It's my first time playing a card-based roguelike with inventory management, and the gameplay setting is really fresh too—it feels so interesting.

The game uses a level-based challenge system. You can play with two character slots, and by using cards, your characters gain skills and equipment. You can also use attack cards to target specific areas. There’s no leveling system during a run. After clearing each stage, you can choose a relic or buy items from the shop. You have four revival chances in total.

The combat doesn’t feel particularly impactful, but you can build combo plays by combining two decks, which is a pretty interesting design. One downside is that if you fail a run completely, you have to start over from scratch. There’s no sense of long-term progression outside the run, like permanent upgrades, and I hope this could be added.

Before we know it, the series has launched its third installment. Its dual strategic mechanics—both pre-battle planning and in-combat tactics—greatly boost the game’s enjoyment. On top of that, the auto-battle feature is an absolute lifesaver; it’s incredibly convenient and takes all the hassle out of fights.

The mechanic of endlessly grinding for artifacts in this game is absolutely brilliant—there are zero duplicate drops, and any artifact you’re chasing can be earned just by putting in the time. On top of that, the dual-character system adds a ton more fun to the gameplay. It’s definitely worth picking up a copy to experience for yourself.
